name: Bor
description: Bor, in Norse mythology, is the father of Odin, Vili, and Vé. He is the son of Búri, the first god, who was licked free from a salty block of ice by the primeval cow Auðumbla. Bor's significance lies primarily in his parentage of the first generation of Æsir gods, who shaped the cosmos from the body of the giant Ymir. While details about Bor himself are sparse in surviving Norse texts, his role as the progenitor of Odin and his brothers solidifies his importance in the Norse creation narrative. Bor's power is essentially ancestral, representing the divine lineage from which the Æsir sprung. He embodies the transition from the primordial state of existence to the structured and ordered world ruled by his sons. Although he isn't actively involved in the myths after the creation, his existence is a crucial link in understanding the origin and authority of the Norse pantheon. His very being represents the burgeoning of divine power from the frozen wastes, a testament to the enduring strength and resilience inherent in the Norse cosmos. He is a foundational figure, the silent architect of a new era, and a reminder of the cyclical nature of creation and destruction in Norse cosmology. Bor's legacy lives on through his powerful sons, whose actions define the destiny of gods and mortals alike. He represents the initial spark of divinity that ignites the entire Norse mythological narrative, a crucial element in understanding the power dynamics and cosmic forces at play. Without Bor, there would be no Odin, no Vili, and no Vé, and consequently, no ordered world as the Norse knew it.
subtitle: Father of Odin, Vili, and Vé

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Bor
keyMyths.description: Bor is born from Búri, who was licked free from a block of ice by the cow Auðumbla. This event marks the beginning of the divine lineage that leads to the Æsir gods.

keyMyths.title: Bor Marries Bestla
keyMyths.description: Bor marries Bestla, the daughter of the giant Bölþorn, and together they have Odin, Vili, and Vé. This union bridges the gap between the gods and the giants.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Bor's sons, Odin, Vili, and Vé, slay the giant Ymir and use his body to create the world. This act establishes the power of the Æsir and the beginning of order.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including the creation story and genealogy of the gods.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, including the creation story and genealogy of the gods.
